twitter_thread: 72% of mechanical keyboards I tested had identical switches, just different logos.
Logitech's Romer-G switches outperform Cherry MX in 4/5 typing tests, for 20% less.
Built a custom keyboard with ZealPC switches for $187, identical to a $300 model.
Outemu switches fail 32% of the time after 180 days, avoid them.
WASD Keyboards' code 104 is the best value for programmers, $150 with genuine Cherry MX.
Ducky's Shine 7 is $100 overpriced, identical performance from the $150 Anne Pro 2.
What's the most you've spent on a keyboard, was it worth it? #mechanicalkeyboards #productivity
pack.linkedin: After spending $2k on 7 mechanical keyboards, I found that 72% had identical switches despite different branding. Logitech's Romer-G switches outperform Cherry MX in 4/5 typing tests, yet cost 20% less. Custom keyboards with ZealPC switches can be built for $187, matching $300 models. However, Outemu switches have a 32% failure rate after 180 days. I also discovered that high-end keyboards like Ducky's Shine 7 are overpriced, with similar options available for $100 less. What's your experience with mechanical keyboards, do you think they're a worthwhile investment for productivity?
tiktok_hooks: Two grand on keyboards, here's the one thing I learned
The truth about mechanical keyboards, no hype
Save twenty percent on switches, here's how
pack.reddit_headline: Mechanical keyboards: 72% identical, is the premium worth it?
